maths.algebra: Algebra involves solving equations using symbols. Linear equations have degree 1, while quadratic equations have degree 2.
maths.indices and logarithms: Indices are powers of numbers. Logarithms are the inverse of indices and help solve exponential equations.
maths.trigonometry: Trigonometry studies relationships between angles and sides of triangles using sine, cosine, and tangent.
maths.geometry: Geometry deals with shapes, sizes, and properties of figures like triangles and circles.
maths.mensuration: Mensuration involves calculating area, volume, and perimeter of shapes.
maths.statistics: Statistics deals with data collection, analysis, mean, median, and mode.
maths.probability: Probability measures the chance of an event occurring.
maths.sets: A set is a collection of objects. Operations include union, intersection, and complement.
maths.variation: Variation shows relationships between variables (direct and inverse).
maths.number bases: Number bases represent numbers in different systems like binary (base 2) and decimal (base 10).
maths.sequences and series: Sequences are ordered numbers; series are sums of sequences.
maths.matrices: Matrices are arrays of numbers used in solving equations.

english.parts of speech: Parts of speech include noun, verb, adjective, adverb, pronoun, preposition, conjunction, and interjection.
english.tenses: Tenses show time of action: past, present, and future.
english.concord: Concord means agreement between subject and verb.
english.lexis and structure: Lexis refers to vocabulary; structure refers to grammar usage.
english.synonyms: Synonyms are words with similar meanings.
english.antonyms: Antonyms are words with opposite meanings.
english.idioms: Idioms are expressions with meanings different from literal interpretation.
english.essay writing: Essay writing involves introduction, body, and conclusion.
english.letter writing: Letter writing can be formal or informal.
english.direct and indirect speech: Direct speech quotes exact words; indirect speech reports them.
english.active and passive voice: Active voice emphasizes subject; passive emphasizes object.

physics.motion: Motion describes movement of objects. It includes speed, velocity, and acceleration.
physics.forces: Force is a push or pull that can change motion.
physics.work energy power: Work is done when force moves an object. Energy is the ability to do work. Power is the rate of doing work.
physics.heat: Heat is energy transfer due to temperature difference.
physics.waves: Waves transfer energy without transferring matter.
physics.light: Light is a form of energy that enables us to see.
physics.electricity: Electricity is flow of electric charge.
physics.magnetism: Magnetism involves magnetic fields and forces.
physics.sound: Sound is a wave produced by vibrations.
physics.pressure: Pressure is force per unit area.
physics.gravitation: Gravitation is the force of attraction between masses.

biology.cell: The cell is the basic unit of life.
biology.nutrition: Nutrition involves how organisms obtain and use food.
biology.reproduction: Reproduction is the process of producing offspring.
biology.ecology: Ecology studies relationships between organisms and environment.
biology.evolution: Evolution explains how organisms change over time.
biology.body systems: Body systems include digestive, respiratory, circulatory systems.
biology.photosynthesis: Photosynthesis is how plants make food using sunlight.

chemistry.atomic structure: Atoms consist of protons, neutrons, and electrons.
chemistry.periodic table: The periodic table organizes elements based on properties.
chemistry.acids bases salts: Acids release H+, bases release OH-, salts are neutral.
chemistry.electrolysis: Electrolysis uses electricity to break compounds.
chemistry.reactions: Chemical reactions involve changes in substances.
chemistry.gas laws: Gas laws describe behavior of gases.
chemistry.rates: Rate of reaction shows how fast reactions occur.
chemistry.equilibrium: Equilibrium is when forward and backward reactions are equal.

government.democracy: Democracy is government by the people.
government.rule of law: Rule of law ensures everyone is equal before the law.
government.separation of powers: Power is divided among branches of government.
government.constitution: A constitution is the supreme law of a country.
government.political parties: Political parties organize to gain power.
government.local government: Local government operates at grassroots level.
government.military rule: Military rule is government by armed forces.
government.international organizations: These include global bodies like the UN.

geography.map reading: Map reading involves interpreting symbols and scales.
geography.population: Population studies human numbers and distribution.
geography.resources: Natural resources are materials from nature.

agricultural science.soil: Soil is essential for crop production.
agricultural science.animal husbandry: Animal husbandry is care of farm animals.
agricultural science.tools: Farm tools help in agricultural activities.

food and nutrition.nutrients: Nutrients include carbohydrates, proteins, fats, vitamins, minerals.
food and nutrition.balanced diet: Balanced diet contains all nutrients in right proportions.
food and nutrition.preservation: Preservation extends shelf life of food.
food and nutrition.cooking methods: Cooking methods include boiling, frying, baking.

financial accounting.final accounts: Final accounts show profit and financial position.
financial accounting.depreciation: Depreciation reduces asset value over time.
